expect: fmea_reconcile.py reads all f-cat1-*.json artifacts, computes per-FM status, outputs authoritative counts. Expect to find 2-3 additional status inconsistencies beyond FM-25.
actual: Built fmea_reconcile.py (500L). Reconciles 41 FMs across 34 artifacts. Found: (1) S489 ADEQUATE count inconsistency (claimed 8, reconciled 9), (2) FM-25 correctly MINIMAL since S475 (confirmed L-1267), (3) FM-29/FM-31 from S426 candidates not previously tracked, (4) FM-40/FM-41 new candidates from S492 text extraction. Distribution: UNMITIGATED=4, MINIMAL=13, PARTIAL=15, ADEQUATE=9. Tool handles 6 artifact types: baseline registry, registry updates, FM-specific hardening, reclassifications, FMEA snapshots, and text-based fallback extraction. Supports --json and --verbose flags.
diff: Expected 2-3 inconsistencies beyond FM-25: GOT 1 formal inconsistency (S489 ADEQUATE count) plus 2 tracking gaps (FM-29/FM-31 from S426 candidates were not in any prior reconciliation). Unexpected: text extraction found FM-40/FM-41 as new candidates — tool discovers FMs that no formal registration process caught. Total FM count 41 matches S492's uncertain estimate of '39-41'.
method: Built reconciliation tool from scratch. Analyzed 34 artifacts across 7 artifact types. Session-ordered processing with per-FM state machine. Status normalization handles legacy and verbose status strings. Text-based fallback catches FMs registered only in prose.
